content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA with length NNN. On each day, the following process was performed:


a new array RRR is created; this array contains all elements AiA_iAi‚Äã such that i=1i=1i=1, i=Ni=Ni=N or min(Ai‚àí1,Ai+1)‚â§Ai\mathop{\rm min}(A_{i-1}, A_{i+1}) \le A_imin(Ai‚àí1‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã)‚â§Ai‚Äã (2‚â§i<N2 \le i \lt N2‚â§i<N), in the original order
AAA is replaced by RRR and NNN is replaced by the length of RRR
In other words, all elements of AAA that are between two bigger elements disappear.

For each element of the original array, calculate the number of the day on which it disappeared (the process starts with day 111), or determine that it never disappeared.
constraints: the sum of NNN in all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points):** the sum of NNN in all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the number of the day on which the iii-th element of the original array disappeared, or 000 if it never disappeared.
